question.text: Which act was passed by the Indian Parliament in 1952 to regulate forward contracts in commodities?

explanation.text: **The Logic:** The Forward Contracts Regulation Act (FCRA) was passed in 1952 by the Parliament to regulate forward contracts in commodities across India. **The Trap:** SCRA 1956 regulates securities contracts, not commodity forwards specifically. The Defence of India Act (1943) was an earlier wartime measure. Essential Supplies Temporary Powers Act (1946) was also earlier. **Cross-Exam Context:** Series VIII covers SCRA. Series III-A covers the broader regulatory framework. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** FCRA was repealed on Sept 28, 2015 when SEBI took over commodity derivatives regulation under SCRA — a landmark unification of financial regulation.
